name: Microgrid node ↔ compute electrical interface — the double bridge
description: The defining transient of the platform: a coherent AI training step hits the bus faster than lean-burn engines can take fuel. The BESS carries the electrical transient while the TCS fluid mass carries the thermal transient — two bridges, one event, one clock.

parameters.notes: 50% of node; conservative — includes thermally-slow cooling load that does not actually step on the fast edge. The §10 correlation exists to verify this number against reality.
5 MW step x 1.20 margin; 4-quadrant — load rejection reverses the problem
energy set by secondary duties; bridge itself ~0.06 MWh across ~45 s ramp

parameters.source: MGN-BOD-002 §5.3 (conservative gas block-load ramp; verify in POC Phase 4)

parameters.notes: at 2 MW from TCS fluid alone (815 L PG25, 15 K margin); ~40–50 s total with CDU buffer + cold plates. The thermal analog of the BESS bridge.

parameters.source: Deschutes Table 18.8.1 (1-second timescale, 3 of 4 feeds operational)

parameters.value: 4 feeds, 3+1 redundancy (4 makes 3); IEC 60309 5-pin 30/32 A 230/240 Vac Wye
5 MW step > N-1 generation loss (2.5 MW) — the load step is the governing case
